app.name: jid
app.one_liner: An interactive CLI tool for exploring JSON with real-time filtering, auto-completion, and JMESPath support.

screens.name: Interactive JSON Explorer
screens.purpose: Display JSON structure and live query results with real-time key highlighting and suggestion prompts.
primary_actions: Cycle through field candidates with Tab
Execute or refine queries with Enter

rust_commands.name: execute_query
rust_commands.purpose: Parse and apply a dot-path or JMESPath query to the current JSON document and return filtered results.

rust_commands.name: highlight_key
rust_commands.purpose: Highlight the JSON key corresponding to the current query segment in the terminal view.

rust_commands.name: load_history
rust_commands.purpose: Load persistent query history from disk with deduplication and limit enforcement.

rust_commands.name: save_history
rust_commands.purpose: Persist executed queries to history file with configurable retention.

rust_commands.name: suggest_fields
rust_commands.purpose: Provide auto-completion candidates based on current query context and JSON structure.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add basic tab-based field suggestion system
week 1: Build terminal rendering loop with real-time key highlighting
week 1: Implement core JSON parsing and dot-path query engine
week 2: Implement persistent query history with deduplication
week 2: Integrate JMESPath support with function templates
acceptance_tests: Configurable keybindings and exit_on_enter option are respected.
JMESPath functions appear as suggestions with auto-filled argument templates.
Query history persists across sessions and respects 1000-entry limit.
Tab key cycles through valid field candidates at current nesting level.
User can type a dot-path query and see matching JSON keys highlighted in real time.
